steps.title: Compare the timelines
steps.content: Alvarez's team found that Polynesian sweet potato varieties diverged from South American ones over 100,000 years ago. But Polynesia was peopled only in the last three thousand years. The plant split from South American varieties long before humans were there.

steps.title: Eliminate human-transfer claims
steps.content: Choice A is unsupported because the text does not date South American cultivation. Choice B contradicts the 100,000-year divergence. Choice D contradicts the wild South American origin and adds a domesticated single variety. The timeline rules out human introduction. The answer is C.
